HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

RWGFun.cpp

5
6#include "RWGFun.h"
7
14RWGFun::RWGFun(SurfaceMesh *inMesh, Triangle *inTri, rvec *inFreeVertex,
15 int inSide)
16 : triangle(inTri), freeVertex(inFreeVertex), mesh(inMesh), side(inSide) {
17 rvec *n1, *n2;
18
19 // Determine edge vertices
20 if (triangle->NodePtr(0) == freeVertex) {
21 n1 = triangle->NodePtr(1);
22 n2 = triangle->NodePtr(2);
23 } else if (triangle->NodePtr(1) == freeVertex) {
24 n1 = triangle->NodePtr(0);
25 n2 = triangle->NodePtr(2);
26 } else {
27 n1 = triangle->NodePtr(0);
28 n2 = triangle->NodePtr(1);
29 }
30
31 // Determine edge index
32 edgeIndex = mesh->FindEdgeFB(n1, n2);
33
34 // If any boundary conditions on the mesh
35 if (edgeIndex == -1) {
36 std::pair<int, SymmetryOperation *> result(mesh->FindEdgeBC(n1, n2));
37 SymmetryOperation *s(result.second);
38
39 // Map the free vertex across the symmetry and append as a new node
40 mesh->AddNode(s->Apply(*freeVertex));
41 freeVertex = mesh->LastNode();
42 edgeIndex = result.first;
43
44 // Ensure (n1, n2) match the edge orientation just resolved
45 std::pair<int, int> d(triangle->BorderingIndeces());
46 double eps(1.e-2);
47 rvec dif(s->Apply(*n1) - *mesh->Edge(edgeIndex).node1);
48 if (dot(dif, dif) < eps) {
49 n1 = mesh->Edge(edgeIndex).node1;
50 n2 = mesh->Edge(edgeIndex).node2;
51 } else {
52 n1 = mesh->Edge(edgeIndex).node2;
53 n2 = mesh->Edge(edgeIndex).node1;
54 }
55
56 // Create the mirrored triangle with consistent orientation
57 rvec normalVector(cross((rvec)(*n2 - *n1), (rvec)(*freeVertex - *n1)));
58 if (dot(normalVector, triangle->FBNormalVector(FRONT)) > 0) {
59 mesh->AddTriangle(n1, n2, freeVertex, d.first, d.second);
60 } else {
61 mesh->AddTriangle(n2, n1, freeVertex, d.first, d.second);
62 }
63 triangle = mesh->LastTriangle();
64 }
65
66 // Determine sign of RWG function
67 if (inSide == FRONT)
68 inverted = false;
69 else
70 inverted = true;
71
72 // Adjust orientation using geometric consistency
73 if (dot(cross(mesh->EdgeVector(edgeIndex), triangle->RefNormalVector()),
74 (rvec)(*n1 - *freeVertex)) < 0)
75 inverted = !inverted;
76}
77
85rvec RWGFun::Evaluate(rvec r) {
86 if (inverted)
87 return (*freeVertex - r) * mesh->EdgeLength(edgeIndex) /
88 (2. * triangle->Area());
89 else
90 return (r - *freeVertex) * mesh->EdgeLength(edgeIndex) /
91 (2. * triangle->Area());
92}
93
94// Owner triangle pointer.
95Triangle *RWGFun::TrianglePtr() { return triangle; }
96
97// Free vertex (by value).
98rvec RWGFun::FreeVertex() { return *freeVertex; }
99
100// Free vertex pointer.
101rvec *RWGFun::FreeVertexPtr() { return freeVertex; }
102
103// True if oriented towards the free vertex.
104bool RWGFun::Inverted() { return inverted; }
105
110double RWGFun::RWGPreFactor() {
111 if (inverted)
112 return -mesh->EdgeLength(edgeIndex) / triangle->Area();
113 else
114 return mesh->EdgeLength(edgeIndex) / triangle->Area();
115}
116
117// Mesh edge index opposite the free vertex.
118int RWGFun::EdgeIndex() { return edgeIndex; }
119
120// Mesh edge structure opposite the free vertex.
121edge RWGFun::Edge() { return mesh->Edge(edgeIndex); }
122
123// Stored side passed at construction (FRONT/BACK).
124int RWGFun::Side() { return side; }

Header for the RWGFun class.

#define FRONT
Orientation tag for the front face of a triangle.
